Title: VvERF045 Integrates Ethylene, Brassinosteroid and Abscisic Acid Pathways to Orchestrate Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Grapevine

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins to form stable anthocyanin 3-O-glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and berry/calli/tomato pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under ETH treatment; transient overexpression/silencing of VvERF045 in berries; stable overexpression/VIGS in grape calli; heterologous tomato overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream structural gene, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvUFGT is a major structural target positively associated with ETH-induced anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Its transcript is induced by VvERF045 overexpression and reduced by VvERF045 silencing. 3. VvERF045 directly binds and activates the VvUFGT promoter, and activation is further enhanced by VvMYB62.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains GCC-box bound by VvERF045.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon treatment; ABA; EBL; BRz; fluridone; 1-MCP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream marker/engineering target for enhancing grape pigmentation, especially together with upstream regulator VvERF045.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the anthocyanin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under ETH treatment; transient overexpression/silencing of VvERF045; stable callus overexpression/VIGS; heterologous tomato overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key downstream structural gene, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvDFR is upregulated by ETH and by VvERF045 overexpression. 2. VvERF045 directly binds the VvDFR promoter through a DRE core-containing region and activates transcription. 3. Co-expression with VvMYB62 synergistically strengthens VvDFR promoter activation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains DRE core motif recognized by VvERF045.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon treatment; ABA; EBL; BRz; fluridone; 1-MCP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural target for improving anthocyanin accumulation when coordinated with upstream TF modules.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole berry; calli; leaves/stems in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous BR accumulation, thereby supporting anthocyanin accumulation and pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under ETH treatment; transient overexpression/silencing of VvERF045; stable callus overexpression/VIGS; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: known BR biosynthetic control enzyme, not explicitly labeled in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes berry coloration indirectly through BR-supported anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvDWF4 transcript is activated by VvERF045 in grape tissues and heterologous tomato. 2. VvERF045 directly binds the VvDWF4 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Upregulation of VvDWF4 is linked with increased BR levels that accompany enhanced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains DRE core variant ACCGAC bound by VvERF045.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target with VvERF045 for improving pigmentation via hormone-supported metabolic flux.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole berry; calli; leaves/stems in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous BR accumulation, supporting anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under ETH treatment; transient overexpression/silencing of VvERF045; stable callus overexpression/VIGS; heterologous tomato overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key BR biosynthetic gene, not explicitly established as rate-limiting in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes berry coloration indirectly through BR-supported anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvCPD is induced by ETH and by VvERF045 overexpression. 2. VvERF045 directly binds and activates the VvCPD promoter. 3. Cooperative activation by the VvERF045-VvMYB62 complex links BR biosynthesis with enhanced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains DRE core variant GCCGAC bound by VvERF045.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for manipulating BR-supported coloration in grape and heterologous crops.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole berry; calli; leaves/stems in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous ABA accumulation, thereby supporting anthocyanin accumulation and pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under ETH treatment; transient overexpression/silencing of VvERF045; stable callus overexpression/VIGS; heterologous tomato overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key ABA biosynthetic node, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes berry coloration indirectly through ABA-supported anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvZEP/ABA1 is induced by ETH and by VvERF045 overexpression across grape systems and tomato. 2. VvERF045 directly binds the VvZEP/ABA1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Co-activation with VvMYB62 strengthens VvZEP/ABA1 promoter activity, linking ABA biosynthesis to anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains DRE core variant TCCGAC bound by VvERF045.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for manipulating ABA-assisted pigmentation and ripening-associated quality traits.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: berry skin; berry pericarp; calli; tomato leaves/stems/fruits in heterologous system
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family, ERF subgroup IV, AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of anthocyanin, brassinosteroid, and abscisic acid
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates VvUFGT, VvDFR, VvDWF4, VvCPD and VvZEP/ABA1; also broadly promotes additional anthocyanin, BR and ABA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central ET-responsive integrator and hub linking ET perception to BR/ABA-dependent anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ethylene/ethephon; also induced by ABA and EBL treatments
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Supports a self-amplifying regulatory circuit integrating ET with BR and ABA pathways
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Berry maturation-associated; strongly induced by ETH across veraison to ripening; expressed in calli
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and berry pigmentation while increasing endogenous BR and ABA levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/WGCNA; qRT-PCR; promoter activation by ETH via Dual-LUC; transient overexpression and silencing in grape berries; stable overexpression and VIGS in grape calli; heterologous tomato overexpression; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC; inhibitor treatments; BiFC/Co-IP/pull-down/Split-LUC for interaction with VvMYB62
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ethephon treatment; EBL; ABA; BRz; fluridone; 1-MCP
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvERF045 is an ET-inducible nuclear ERF transcription factor identified as a hub gene correlated with anthocyanin and BR biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increases, and silencing decreases, anthocyanin, BR and ABA levels together with pigmentation. 3. VvERF045 directly binds and activates promoters of VvUFGT, VvDFR, VvDWF4, VvCPD and VvZEP/ABA1. 4. It physically interacts with VvMYB62, and the complex synergistically enhances target promoter activation. 5. Chemical inhibition of BR or ABA biosynthesis attenuates the VvERF045 overexpression phenotype, placing VvERF045 upstream of BR/ABA-assisted anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ETH rapidly promotes both VvERF045 promoter activity and protein accumulation/stability in Nicotiana benthamiana. In tomato overexpression lines, dwarfism and larger fruit suggest elevated BR-related developmental outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target for precision improvement of grape coloration and anthocyanin enrichment; suitable for ET-responsive engineering or stacking with VvMYB62, while monitoring BR-related architecture effects.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of anthocyanin and support of BR/ABA biosynthesis via cooperative regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: co-activation through protein interaction with VvERF045
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances VvERF045-mediated activation of VvDFR, VvUFGT, VvDWF4, VvCPD and VvZEP/ABA1; overexpression also upregulates anthocyanin pathway genes and VvDWF4/VvZEP/ABA1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cooperative MYB partner that potentiates VvERF045-driven anthocyanin, BR and ABA biosynthetic outputs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ethylene/ethephon; VvERF045 positively correlates with and promotes its expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Berry-specific predominant expression; induced from veraison to maturation; ET-inducible
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin-associated gene expression and strengthens VvERF045-dependent activation of anthocyanin/BR/ABA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics/co-expression; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; promoter activation by ETH via Dual-LUC; overexpression in grape calli; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pull-down; Split-LUC; combinatorial Dual-LUC with VvERF045
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYB62 is an ET-inducible berry-enriched R2R3-MYB co-expressed with VvERF045. 2. It physically interacts with VvERF045 in the nucleus, as shown by Y2H, BiFC, Co-IP, pull-down and Split-LUC. 3. VvMYB62 alone promotes expression of anthocyanin-related genes and some BR/ABA biosynthetic genes, but does not show direct promoter binding/activation in yeast. 4. In combination with VvERF045, it strongly boosts activation of VvDFR, VvUFGT, VvDWF4, VvCPD and VvZEP/ABA1 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper supports VvMYB62 as a cooperative co-activator rather than autonomous direct DNA-binding activator of the tested targets in this system.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising partner for stacking with VvERF045 to enhance grape pigmentation and associated hormone-supported pathways.
